frames.name: Human tragedy and rescue operations
frames.description: Focuses on personal stories of loss, rescue efforts, and the immediate human impact of the disaster
frames.emphasis: Individual victims' stories, family grief, rescue worker efforts, and community response

frames.name: Emergency response and government action
frames.description: Highlights official response measures, emergency declarations, and government assistance
frames.emphasis: State of emergency declarations, presidential response, humanitarian assistance, and official casualty figures

frames.name: Meteorological record-breaking event
frames.description: Presents the disaster as an unprecedented weather event with specific rainfall measurements
frames.emphasis: Record rainfall statistics (584mm in February), wettest February on record, extreme weather context

frames.name: Systemic vulnerability and inequality
frames.description: Frames the disaster within broader patterns of social vulnerability and inadequate public support
frames.emphasis: Lack of government support for communities, higher risk for poorer populations, systemic issues

frames.name: Climate change context
frames.description: Connects the event to broader climate change patterns and extreme weather trends
frames.emphasis: Scientists' attribution to climate change, increasing severity and frequency of extreme weather

outlierPerspectives: Regional context linking to Peru's simultaneous flooding emergency
Explicit connection between poverty and disaster vulnerability
Scientific attribution to climate change as a contributing factor
missingContext: Historical flood patterns in Minas Gerais region
Infrastructure and urban planning factors that may have contributed to vulnerability
Comparison with previous major flooding events in Brazil
Details about early warning systems and their effectiveness
Specific geological factors that made landslides more likely
